answers.body:
@Transactional will work as expected only under Spring-managed thread context. As Quartz job executes in its own thread, rollback doesn't happen as expected.

Quartz job can be made sure that it is Spring managed. Also, transactional logic can be moved to services layer instead of placing @Transactional on the job class

title: Transactions are not rollbacking in quartz when spring @Transactional is used
body:
I am using @Transactional annotation and inside it doing Quartz scheduling (used JobStoreCMT Jobstore specified in quartz.properties file) if any exception thrown after scheduleJob() method then it doesn't delete rows from JobDetails and JobTrigger Table of Quartz as those jobs are not triggered yet.

I want Job and Triggers details to be deleted from table when some exception is thrown after scheduleJob.

@Transactional

void rollback() ..

  1. called quartz scheduler
  2. throw Exception
